compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
DERISK (E4): the submit-requirement predicate is EMPIRICALLY VALID against live Gerrit 3.14.1 — GET /changes/?q=label:LLM-Review=MAX AND -has:unresolved returns HTTP 200 (parses), as do has:unresolved / -has:unresolved / label:Code-Review=MAX. Concrete config to apply (doc-verified syntax):
[label "LLM-Review"]
  function = NoBlock
  value = -1 LLM flagged blocking issues
  value =  0 No score
  value = +1 LLM approved
  defaultValue = 0
  copyCondition = changekind:TRIVIAL_REBASE OR changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE
[submit-requirement "LLM-Review"]
  submittableIf = label:LLM-Review=MAX AND -has:unresolved
submitWholeTopic is server config: gerrit.config [change] submitWholeTopic = true (set in S2's gerrit.config or here). New labels must use function=NoBlock (blocking functions are rejected; gating is via the submit-requirement).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## S3 implemented + verified LIVE on Gerrit rebar.solutions.navateam.com — 2026-06-30 (commit ca05de9d9)

The `rebar` project's refs/meta/config carries the LLM-Review label + submit requirement + ACL; submitWholeTopic is server-global. Applied via setup-project.sh (after granting Administrators refs/meta/config push on All-Projects, server-side, so the normal admin SSH push path works).

**Live config (verified via REST):**
- Label `LLM-Review`: function=NoBlock, values -1..+1, copyCondition=changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE.
- Submit requirement `LLM-Review`: submittableIf = `label:LLM-Review=MAX AND -has:unresolved`.
- Submit requirement `Code-Review`: applicability_expression = `is:false` (DISABLED on rebar) — so LLM-Review is the SOLE applicable gate (bot-only submit, no human Code-Review in v1).
- ACL: label-LLM-Review granted ONLY to Service Users + Administrators.
- `change.submitWholeTopic = true` in server gerrit.config [change].

**AC verification (subagent-driven, all PASS):**
- AC1 — submit blocked until LLM-Review=MAX: a change created via refs/for/master is UNSATISFIED/non-submittable; after admin LLM-Review=+1 -> SATISFIED. (The `-has:unresolved` predicate is present in config; the MAX gate conclusively proven.)
- AC2 — label exclusive: admin (Administrators) can vote; a regular account (1000001 devuser, Registered Users only) vote was REJECTED with `Applying label "LLM-Review": 1 is restricted`.
- AC3 — copyCondition: a commit-message-only amend (NO_CODE_CHANGE) CARRIED the LLM-Review=+1 to the new patchset; a real rebase onto a moved base (reported TRIVIAL_REBASE, parent 9f3d69e->8891672) DROPPED the vote -> re-review required. Exactly the intended NO_CODE_CHANGE-only behavior.
- AC4 — submitWholeTopic: changes 22 & 23 on topic `rebar-test`; submitting 22 alone was rejected ("must be submitted with change 23 ... not ready"); after approving both, a single submit MERGED both atomically.
- AC5 — single LLM-Review gate: ADR-0013 records the deliberate single-gate stance (Code-Review override making it non-applicable) + planned evolution (epic 1fa8).
- Bonus: bot-only submit verified — change 24 with ONLY LLM-Review=MAX shows Code-Review=NOT_APPLICABLE, LLM-Review=SATISFIED (sole applicable gate satisfied).

**Code review (subagent):** VERDICT PASS (no blockers; NITs folded: grep -qxF fixed-string, untracked-groups no-op detection via git add -A + diff --cached, accurate header comment, -1-not-a-veto note in ADR, groups file now includes Registered Users via global:Registered-Users).

The "only the bot votes" end-to-end proof completes in S4 (the bot Service-User account is created there); S3's vote was exercised by an admin (Administrators has the grant) and a regular user was proven unable to vote.

compiled_state.description:
# S3 — Gerrit project + LLM-Review label + submit requirement + submitWholeTopic

## Why
This is the gate's integrity rule: a change is submittable ONLY when the deterministic
`LLM-Review` vote is MAX and there are no unresolved comments. `copyCondition` avoids re-paying
for an expensive LLM review on a no-code-change re-upload; `submitWholeTopic` lands whole
reviewed features atomically. The integrity property is not just "the label gates submit" but
"ONLY the bot can set the label" — so a human cannot cast `LLM-Review` and bypass the gate.
Gerrit is pinned to **3.14.1** (d251 docker-compose), so every predicate/feature used here
(`-has:unresolved`, `copyCondition`, submit requirements) is above its minimum and was
validated to parse against live 3.14.1.

## What
- Create the `rebar` project in Gerrit (NoteDb).
- `LLM-Review` label in `refs/meta/config` (project.config): `function=NoBlock`; values `-1..+1`;
  **`copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE`** (peer-pattern correction). We deliberately do
  NOT include `changekind:TRIVIAL_REBASE`: a TRIVIAL_REBASE copies the vote even when the change
  was rebased onto a **different base** (diff is byte-identical but the base moved), which would
  certify a tree the LLM never reviewed against the new base — the "copyCondition certifies an
  unreviewed tree" risk the Opus red-team flagged. `NO_CODE_CHANGE` pins the parent tree, so it
  only carries the vote across a true no-op re-upload (e.g. commit-message-only edit). A real
  rebase therefore drops the vote and forces a fresh review (the safe default for a correctness
  gate). `function=NoBlock` is **required, not a free choice**: on 3.14.1 a blocking label
  function is rejected, so blocking comes from the submit requirement.
- Submit requirement (project.config): `submittableIf = label:LLM-Review=MAX AND -has:unresolved`.
  This is what blocks submit; the label is advisory on its own. Both predicates validated to
  parse (HTTP 200) on live Gerrit 3.14.1.
- `change.submitWholeTopic=true` — set in the **server-level** `gerrit.config` `[change]`
  section, NOT in project.config (it is a server-global key, silently ignored in project.config).
  GOTCHA (peer research): topics are **global + un-namespaced**, so a topic name reused in a repo
  the submitter can't see could silently block submit; for this single-repo PoC that risk is
  minimal, but adopt a topic naming convention (e.g. `rebar-<feature>`) and document it.
- Access controls: grant the auto-generated `label-LLM-Review` vote permission to the **Service
  Users** group; humans may push changes for review but cannot submit without the label and
  cannot themselves cast `LLM-Review`. The Service Users group is created/wired in **S4**; S3
  lands config that references it and is testable in S3 by an **admin** casting the vote.

## Scope
In: project + `LLM-Review` label (NO_CODE_CHANGE copyCondition) + submit requirement +
server-level topic config + the label ACL grant. The "only the bot votes" end-to-end proof is
completed in S4 (the bot account does not exist in S3); in S3 the vote is exercised by an admin.
Out: the bot account + event/vote wiring (S4); replication (S5); GitHub lockdown (S6). Gerrit
version is fixed upstream by d251 (3.14.1).

## Files
- `infra/gerrit/project.config` (pushed to `refs/meta/config`): label, submit requirement,
  `copyCondition=changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE`, ACL grant.
- `infra/gerrit/gerrit.config` (server-level): `[change]\n  submitWholeTopic = true`. (Impl
  note: same gerrit.config d251 mounts; S3 owns/patches the `[change]` section.)
- `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`: provisions the project + pushes `refs/meta/config`. Impl
  notes (not separate ACs): (a) **idempotent** — push a fully-declarative project.config via
  `git push HEAD:refs/meta/config`, guard project creation with check-before-create; (b)
  **auth** — needs Gerrit admin creds (HTTP password / SSH key), source documented (env/SSM),
  fail-fast if absent; (c) **dry-run/diff** mode before mutating the live instance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A change can be pushed for review to the `rebar` project; submit is BLOCKED until
      `LLM-Review=+1` (MAX) AND no unresolved comments — verified by an admin casting the vote
      (the bot-only path is proven in S4). Both predicates parse + enforce on 3.14.1.
- [ ] The `label-LLM-Review` vote permission is granted **exclusively** to the Service Users
      group: a regular developer cannot cast `LLM-Review` (push `%l=LLM-Review+1` is rejected /
      not applied) while a Service-Users member can.
- [ ] `copyCondition=changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE` carries the vote across a no-code-change
      re-upload (e.g. commit-message-only amend) BUT a genuine rebase onto a moved base
      (TRIVIAL_REBASE) does NOT carry it — the vote is dropped and re-review is required.
      Verified on 3.14.1 by (a) a message-only amend keeping the vote and (b) a real rebase
      dropping it.
- [ ] `change.submitWholeTopic=true` is set in server-level `gerrit.config` `[change]` (NOT
      project.config), and demonstrated: two changes under one topic submit atomically and
      neither submits independently while its topic partner is open.
- [ ] The submit requirement is the SINGLE code-review vote in v1 (no human Code-Review / CI
      Verified yet — follow-on epic 1fa8). Record this deliberate single-gate stance + planned
      evolution in a brief ADR/note.

file_impact.reason: rebar project ACL + LLM-Review label + submit requirement
ADR for single LLM-Review label + submit requirement

compiled_state.title: infra: S3 — Gerrit project + LLM-Review label + submit requirement + submitWholeTopic
